def: Absence of menstruation ≥3 missed periods; primary (never started by 16) vs. secondary (stopped ≥6 months after being established).
Painful menstruation limiting activity; primary (no pathology) vs. secondary (underlying pelvic disorder).
Descent of the uterus into the vagina from relaxed uterosacral/cardinal ligaments, graded first–fourth degree.
Bladder protrusion into the anterior vaginal wall from weakened vaginal musculature.
Rectal protrusion into the posterior vaginal wall.
Infection of the oviducts/ovaries and adjacent organs — cervicitis, endometritis, salpingitis, oophoritis.
Inflammation of the vulva and vagina, most often fromCandida.
The most common benign uterine tumor — estrogen-sensitive smooth muscle growth.
An ectopic focus of endometrial tissue outside the uterine cavity — the hallmark of endometriosis.
Preeclampsia-eclampsia — rapid BP rise + proteinuria in pregnancy.
Placenta implanted over the internal cervical os.
Premature separation of the placenta before delivery.
Chronic periductal inflammation with dilated, debris-filled subareolar ducts — can mimic malignancy.
Cyclic, hormone-related palpable breast masses/tenderness — not a distinct disease, and mostly NOT a cancer risk factor.
